one_line_pitch: An alarm clock that runs your life as a shonen anime: the alarm is the cold open, standing up mints the episode, and missing a morning is a canon chapter — never a dead streak.
winning_concept: behavior-science ("Cold Open") wins the spine. Three lenses tied at 7.3, but behavior-science is the only one whose anime layer changes MECHANICS, not paint: the time-aware Home state machine (judges called it "the single best intuitiveness decision in the whole document"), the Cliffhanger Teaser (a Zeigarnik open loop that makes users curious about their own alarm — category-creating), the Title Card Slam (the highest-rated organic-share engine across all 15 verdicts), and failure-as-canon Knockdown Chapters (the only genuinely new answer to the month-2 churn cliff). dead-simple-utility and apple-craft scored equal but are execution disciplines, not products — so we keep behavior-science's narrative engine and import their discipline wholesale.
grafted_ideas: [dead-simple-utility] Onboarding IS the core action: first screen is a giant time picker with one gold BEGIN — setting the alarm is the entire first run, voice and quest defaulted, no carousel, no quiz, no account wall. (Judge: 'best-in-class first-run.')
[dead-simple-utility] The 'one gold element per screen' contract: exactly one accent-colored primary action exists on any screen, ever — an enforceable rule, not a vibe.

[dead-simple-utility] The Narrator Remembers: every episode is generated against the user's actual behavior log (grace uses, early wakes, near-broken arcs) so day-30 content is structurally about THEM — the anti-staleness moat all three judges flagged as the most stealable retention mechanic.

[apple-craft] The Dawn Rail: Alarm → Quest → Title Card → Episode → Card is a forced linear modal pipeline with zero nav chrome — two brains, two modes; the 6:30am brain literally cannot get lost because no other screens exist.

[apple-craft] Truth-based card rarity: foils earned only by verifiable real-world conditions — woke before sunrise (Daybreaker), bad-weather wake (Storm Riser), beat-your-alarm (First Light) — honest variable reward that can't be gamed by copy.
[apple-craft] The Ember: lock-screen Live Activity + StandBy night clock with a dimming ember that flares 10 min before the alarm — the nightstand phone is in-universe before the app opens.

[anime-native] Episode count, not streak: the headline number (EP 212) is additive and unbreakable at the data-model level — pride, never loss-anxiety. Arcs and knockdowns layer narrative on top, but nothing ever resets to zero.
[anime-native] Snooze as a visible, priced narrative choice: 'FILLER (snooze 9 min)' is a real labeled button, not a hidden hold gesture — escape is honest, the cost is story.
[social-viral] Onboarding-as-ritual: the first-run alarm setup IS the nightly Next Episode flow, so nothing is ever learned twice.
[social-viral] The Live Activity reframe: every nighttime doom-scroll glance at the lock screen shows 'EP 48 airs in 7h 14m' — hijacking the exact behavior that causes the problem.
core_loop: NIGHT (optional, 30s, never required): At wind-down time, one push: "Episode 48 is being written." Home is already in Night state: alarm time huge with a toggle (always armed by default — no nightly arming ritual, missing the ritual NEVER silences the alarm), tomorrow's quest chip pre-selected from yesterday, and one optional prompt: "Tomorrow's mission?" (voice-first mic button or 3 suggestion chips; skippable in one tap — skipping costs episode specificity, never guilt). Closing the app fires the 1.5s TO BE CONTINUED stinger and the generated Cliffhanger Teaser line ("Tomorrow the rival finds out about the essay…"). Episode audio generates server-side overnight; mornings need zero network. ALARM (6:30): Critical alert fires into the Dawn Rail takeover. 8s personalized Cold Open in the narrator's voice using their name and real history ("Oi, Maya. Day 48. You bought ninety seconds yesterday — was it worth it?") before the tone escalates. One giant crimson button: BEGIN QUEST. Below it, smaller but VISIBLE: "FILLER (snooze 9 min)" — honest, labeled, costs a grey panel. Week 1 additionally shows a plain-language safety line ("Sick? Hold here to end the alarm"). QUEST (30–90s): One instruction in 64pt, max 5 words; the sensor IS the screen (viewfinder with halftone frame, giant step counter, shake ring). Verification has a designed failure ladder: 2 failed verifies auto-offers a simpler fallback quest (20 shakes standing); 3 fails ends the alarm and logs a Filler — the alarm NEVER traps. On verify: white flash, heavy haptic, hard cut — the cut IS the confirmation. PAYOFF (90s, skippable from second zero): TITLE CARD SLAM — "EPISODE 48: THE ESSAY DEMON," generated from their own night input, smash-cut at 12° with halftone burst. Morning Episode auto-plays with anime subtitles (works on mute); SKIP is dim but present from frame one, and a 20s Short Mode exists for late mornings. Then the Wake Card mints: quest photo screentone-filtered into a manga panel, episode number, wake time, truth-based foil if earned. SHARE (9:16 export with the slam baked in) or it auto-files to Saga. Then the app tells them to leave: "Go. The episode is live." DAY (pull only, zero daytime pushes ever): Home shows one giant LOCK IN button with a first-week sublabel ("20s of hype before gym/study/work"); tap → 4 context chips → instant 25s clip, hold-to-exit AND a visible X. EVENING: loop closes. FAILURE: a missed morning is a KNOCKDOWN — a red-ink canon chapter, never a reset. Tomorrow opens with a Comeback Quest one notch harder; landing it makes the knockdown a plot beat. Earned Recovery Days (1 per 7-day run, max 2) auto-absorb misses. A fully dead arc archives as a finished volume on the shelf and Arc N+1 begins immediately. Episode count only ever goes up.
information_architecture: Two-mode architecture for two brains. MODE 1 — DAWN RAIL (80% of usage): Alarm → Quest → Title Card → Episode → Card as a forced linear modal pipeline launched by the alarm, zero nav chrome, no back button, no tab bar — forward is the only direction. MODE 2 — THE APP, exactly 3 tabs: (1) TODAY (default) — a time-aware state machine rendering Morning (episode replay + mission + fresh card), Day (giant LOCK IN), Night (End Credits inline + teaser), or Post-Miss (Comeback framing). FIX for the judges' shape-shifting complaint — two persistent anchors survive every state: the alarm time + toggle is ALWAYS visible and tappable top-center (opens the Alarm Sheet at any hour), and LOCK IN is ALWAYS reachable (full button by day, compact pill in other states). The clock changes emphasis, never removes capabilities. (2) SAGA — the identity shelf: episode count huge at top, arcs as bound manga volume spines, the card binder grid, knockdown chapters in red ink, season finale dividers. (3) CAST — narrator roster (fighting-game select with instant 5s voice samples), arc packs, settings gear, and the Protagonist Pass paywall sheet (never a tab). Plain-English labels everywhere in week 1: the snooze says "snooze," CAST not "Vault," no "Cour Pass." Lock-screen Live Activity (episode countdown + ember), StandBy ember clock, and a home widget carry ambient surface area so the app stays small. No feed, no friends, no inbox — the rival is a character, not a leaderboard; sharing flows outward to TikTok/IG only.

key_screens.job: Teach the whole product and complete setup in under 15 seconds with one decision.
key_screens.layout: Black screen, film grain, low narrator audio: 'You've snoozed through 4,000 mornings. This isn't one of them.' Hard cut + haptic thud → title card slam: 'EPISODE 0: THE ONE WHO COULDN'T WAKE UP.' Then one screen: 'WHAT TIME DOES YOUR STORY START?' in 28pt over a giant wheel time picker, one gold BEGIN button. Voice (Mentor) and quest (Get Up) are defaulted; name capture and notification/critical-alert permission are folded into the same flow with one line of plain copy each. No carousel, no account wall. This screen IS the nightly scheduler they'll see forever — nothing is learned twice.

key_screens.job: Get a half-asleep human vertical with one tap — and never trap anyone.
key_screens.layout: Full-bleed OLED black, no status bar. Top: 'EP. 48' pulsing in 96pt condensed italic with the Cold Open subtitle line rendered anime-style (white, 2px stroke) syncing to audio. Bottom 45%: one full-width crimson BEGIN QUEST slab, thumb-reachable from a pillow. Below it, 40% opacity but clearly legible: 'FILLER (snooze 9 min)' — tapping plays one rival line, dims the ember, greys today's panel. Days 1–7 only: a third line, 'Sick or can't do this? Hold to end alarm' (3s hold, ends everything, logs a Recovery Day if banked). Screen brightness ramps 20%→100% over 20s. No other elements exist.

key_screens.job: Verify one physical action with zero reading and a designed failure ladder.
key_screens.layout: Top: instruction in 64pt heavy caps, max 5 words ('PHOTOGRAPH THE SKY') + one pictogram. The sensor IS the remaining 75%: full-screen viewfinder with halftone frame and a shutter occupying the bottom quarter, OR a 120pt live counter (14/20) inside a filling ink-stroke ring. After 2 failed verifications a fallback card slides up: 'CAN'T VERIFY — 20 SHAKES INSTEAD' (one tap swaps quest); a 3rd failure ends the alarm and logs Filler. On success: 1-frame white flash, 4px shake, heavy haptic, smash-cut. No dialogs, no retake menus, no dead ends.

key_screens.job: Deliver the daily dopamine spike and frame the mission — the screenshot moment.
key_screens.layout: Black, then the generated title slams in at 12° with halftone burst: 'EPISODE 48: THE ESSAY DEMON' over a daily generated sunrise-palette background, holds 2.5s, auto-saves a clean frame. Settles into the player: narrator flat-cel portrait with 2-frame idle loop, gold waveform, subtitles line-by-line in the lower third (current line white, past lines 35%) so it works on mute. Controls: 64pt play/pause, SKIP visible from second zero, 'Short' chip (20s cut) for rushed mornings. Final 10s: today's mission line types on and persists; auto-advances to the Card Mint. Audio survives backgrounding via Dynamic Island — they WILL open TikTok; plan for it.

key_screens.job: Mint today's proof artifact and invite (never demand) the share.
key_screens.layout: Card flips in center at 70% height with one foil-shine pass and a 'shing': the real quest photo screentone-filtered into a manga panel (the filter is load-bearing — it makes a grim 6:30am bedroom shareable, and on dark winter mornings the generated title-card art becomes the backdrop instead so no card is ever an ugly black rectangle), episode number stamped like a chapter mark, wake time huge, receipt-style mono footer (weather, arc day). Truth-based foils (First Light, Daybreaker, Storm Riser) shimmer with gyro. One gold SHARE (exports 9:16 with the 3s slam baked in as video) + 'Done' text button. Done → Today. Card files to Saga regardless.

key_screens.job: Show the ONE right action for this moment — with anchors that never move.
key_screens.layout: Persistent anchors in every state: top-center alarm time + toggle (tap → half-height Alarm Sheet: oversized wheel, quest chips with one-word sensor notes, voice chips, SAVE), top-left 'ARC II · EP 48' chip → Saga. DAY: massive circular crimson LOCK IN button (60% width, 4s breathing), mission line above, today's card peeking at bottom. NIGHT: End Credits inline — one stamp row (CLEARED / SURVIVED / KNOCKED DOWN), mic-first mission field with 3 chips, then the Cliffhanger Teaser types itself under 'NEXT EPISODE…'; LOCK IN compresses to a pill bottom-right. MORNING: replay tile + mission + minted card. POST-MISS: Comeback Quest framing, red ink. No feed, no stats grid.

key_screens.job: Deliver 25 seconds of context hype in two taps, one-handed, gloves on.
key_screens.layout: LOCK IN → third-height sheet, four oversized chips: GYM · STUDY · WORK · RESET, each with an ink-brush glyph. One tap plays immediately: full-bleed context color, narrator portrait, kinetic captions of key lines, live waveform. Exits: visible X top-right AND hold-anywhere (the invisible-affordance trap is fixed). Optional 'Start a block' chip spawns a Dynamic Island timer ('LOCKED IN — 47:12'). Free: 1/day, counter shown only after it's spent; premium: unlimited + all voices.

key_screens.job: Turn 30 mornings into identity evidence the user refuses to abandon.
key_screens.layout: Top: episode count enormous ('EP 212') — additive, unbreakable. Below: horizontal shelf of bound Volume spines (one per 14-day arc) with generated covers; current arc's spine fills as it progresses, finale slot gilded. Tap a volume → vertical scroll of daily pages: title, card thumbnail, wake time; knockdowns in red ink with torn edges (canon, visible, never hidden); Filler days grey-toned, no red, no shame copy. Every finale: an arc-summary card ('ARC I: THE ONE WHO COULDN'T WAKE UP — 14 EPISODES, 2 KNOCKDOWNS, 1 COMEBACK') plus a one-time 2-minute finale episode narrating their real stats. Segmented toggle: Timeline / Binder (card grid, foils glinting) / Rival (taunt log — opt-out lives here too).

key_screens.job: Sell 'who narrates your life,' demonstrated not described.
key_screens.layout: Fighting-game roster: Mentor (free, unlocked), Rival, Captain, Quiet Senior, monthly guest — full-color cards with a small lock, never greyed husks. Tapping any card instantly plays a 5s sample speaking the user's REAL name and episode count — the demo IS the pitch. Sheet rises on locked-tap or after Episode 3's title card (sell after the magic, never before): three plain lines (personalized daily episodes with callbacks · full cast + custom arcs · unlimited Lock Ins + foil odds), annual pre-selected with trial badge, monthly, restore. Loss-framed around the fully-personalized 3-day Pilot Arc they already heard. No timers, no slashed prices. Free tier forever keeps: the alarm, one quest type, Mentor voice, weekly (not daily) generated episodes, and the card mint — the narrator never fully stops remembering, so the core promise survives non-payment.

design_language: INK-AND-SIGNAL, DARK-FIRST — the app lives at 10:30pm and 6:30am, both in the dark. COLOR: base #0B0E14 (OLED-true ink navy), surfaces #161B26, paper-white text #F2EFE6 (warm, easier at 6am than pure white). ONE signal color per job, strictly rationed: Shonen Crimson #FF2E4C for the single primary action on any screen (alarm, quest, Lock In); Ember Gold #FFC93C reserved EXCLUSIVELY for rarity, foils, and milestones so gold keeps slot-machine meaning; knockdown red-ink #B3122E for canon failures; one semantic green #3DDC84 only for quest verification. No gradients except foil passes and the daily generated sunrise title-card backgrounds. Rule: one accent element per screen, ever. TYPE: two faces, hard limit. Display = ultra-condensed heavy grotesque (Anton-class), ALL-CAPS, −6° skew, used ONLY for episode numbers, title cards, and card text. UI = clean geometric sans (Inter), 17pt minimum body, 700 weight for actions, tabular oversized numerals for times. Spoken audio always gets classic anime subtitle treatment: white, 2px black stroke, lower third. SHAPE: manga, not bubbly — 4px radii on panels/cards with 2px ink borders and 12° diagonal slash dividers; native 20pt continuous corners on functional sheets and chrome. Panels never get blur; chrome never gets panels. Buttons are full-width slabs, 56pt minimum on any pre-9am screen. Halftone screentone at 8–10% on imagery and celebration surfaces only. MOTION: anime editing grammar — hard cuts, never crossfades, for hero moments; the SMASH CUT (6 frames of radial speed-lines, panel scales 1.15→1.0 with overshoot, 1-frame white flash, heavy haptic) and the CARD MINT (single confident snap + foil sweep + gyro parallax) are the only two signature transitions; the Lock In button breathes at 4s; loading states are film-leader countdowns; Reduce Motion swaps cuts for dissolves. HAPTICS: a composed score, not default taps — Power-Up (three rising transients + heavy thud) on quest verify, Mint (crisp snap), Rival (one low rumble on Filler), Ember (soft 0.5Hz pre-alarm). Flutter: platform channel to CoreHaptics with AHAP files; takeovers via SystemUiMode.immersive; slams are AnimatedScale+Opacity with a white Container flash — no Lottie for v1; screentone is one tiled PNG blend overlay. ILLUSTRATION: narrators as high-craft flat-cel 2D portraits with 2-frame idle loops (blink, breath) — faces, not silhouettes (anime-natives simp for characters; brutalist mystery boxes read as cosplay), but zero chibi mascots, zero clip-art, zero confetti, ever. User photos always pass the screentone filter. SOUND: foley on every confirm (taiko thud, blade shing for foils), distinct rival leitmotif; episode audio is the star, UI sound supports it. VOICE/COPY: 'taunt or salute, never nag' — second-person, short, references real history; rival tone has a hard guardrail: never taunts during knockdown recovery, and a Rival-intensity setting (off/light/full) lives in Cast.
signature_moments: THE TITLE CARD SLAM — 10 seconds after you stand up: hard cut, haptic thud, and a generated anime episode title for YOUR day ('EPISODE 48: THE ESSAY DEMON'), built from what you said last night, smash-cuts onto a daily sunrise background. The most screenshot-able second in the app, every single morning, exported as a 3s 9:16 video in one tap.

THE COLD OPEN WITH RECEIPTS — the alarm doesn't start with a tone; it starts with 8 seconds of your narrator saying your actual name, your episode count, and a callback to yesterday's real behavior ('You bought ninety seconds yesterday. Was it worth it?'). 'My alarm just called me out by name' is a self-recording TikTok.

THE CLIFFHANGER TEASER + TO BE CONTINUED — every night ends with a generated 'NEXT EPISODE…' line about tomorrow and a 1.5s black stinger as you close the app. You go to sleep mid-story; the only way to resolve the loop is to wake up. The app's best exit moment happens when you leave it.

THE KNOCKDOWN CHAPTER — miss a morning and nothing resets: the miss is written into your Saga as a red-ink canon chapter, and tomorrow opens with the comeback ('Every protagonist eats the floor in episode 12. What matters is episode 13.'). Failure becomes content; a dead arc archives as a finished volume, never a zero.

FIRST LIGHT FOILS — truth-based rarity: wake before your alarm fires and the card mints holographic with gyro shine; storm wakes and pre-sunrise wakes earn their own foils. The rarest behaviors produce the rarest collectibles, so users start racing their own alarm — the terminal form of the habit.

THE EMBER — from lights-out to dawn, a tiny ember lives in the Dynamic Island and StandBy clock, dimming through the night and flaring 10 minutes before the alarm, while the lock screen counts down 'EP 48 airs in 7h 14m.' Every doom-scroll glance now shows the story instead of guilt.
what_we_kill: The hidden 10-second hold-to-surrender (behavior-science) — every judge flagged it as a panic moment and 1-star generator. Escape is now visible, labeled, and honest: FILLER snooze always on screen, plain-language sick-day hold during week 1.
Unskippable / no-scrubber episode playback (behavior-science, anime-native) — forced 120s audio is hostile by month 2. SKIP exists from second zero plus a 20s Short Mode; skipping never punishes.

Verification dead-ends (all concepts were silent) — an undismissable alarm with a failing camera at 6:31am is the worst failure mode in the category. We ship the failure ladder: 2 fails → fallback quest, 3 fails → alarm ends, Filler logged. The alarm never traps.
Breakable streaks as the headline number (behavior-science's 'arcs end') — replaced by anime-native's additive episode count; arcs and knockdowns are narrative layers on a number that only goes up.

Mandatory nightly arming and homework (dead-simple's 'Lights out', anime-native's required End Credits) — the alarm is always armed by default; the night ritual is a 30s optional enhancer whose only cost is episode specificity.

The social party layer (social-viral) — Morning Panels, public Cracked Cards, and friend-dependent retention: brutal cold-start, public shame in disguise, and BeReal's proven decay curve. The rival is a CHARACTER with memory, not a leaderboard of friends. Sharing flows outward only.
Public failure shame (social-viral's Cracked Cards visible to the party) — knockdowns are private canon in YOUR saga; the rival never taunts during recovery, and rival intensity is user-adjustable.
Silhouette-only narrators (anime-native) — anime-natives want faces to attach to; we commission flat-cel character art instead of brutalist mystery boxes (and never bootleg existing IP).

Jargon-gated day 1 (all concepts) — 'Cour Pass', 'Vault', unlabeled 'LOCK IN', morphing buttons. Week 1 ships plain sublabels, the word 'snooze' appears, CAST not Vault, and the home screen's two anchors (alarm time, Lock In) never disappear across states.
Dashboard IA and 5-tab layouts (the prior over-structured attempts) — three tabs, two modes, one gold action per screen. If a screen needs explaining, it gets cut.
Timer-and-slashed-price paywall gimmicks — voice samples speaking your real name and episode count ARE the pitch; utility-app trust converts this demographic, desperation doesn't.
build_order: 1. ALARM ENGINE + DAWN RAIL SKELETON — reliable critical-alert alarm in Flutter (iOS first), full-screen takeover route stack (Alarm → Quest → Title → Episode → Card) with zero chrome, the visible FILLER snooze, and the week-1 sick-day escape. This is the trust floor; nothing else matters if the alarm flakes. Validate the degraded path (notification-tap → app open) since full lock-screen takeover has OS limits.

2. WAKE QUEST VERIFICATION + FAILURE LADDER — Get Up (motion), Sky Photo (camera + liveness), Shake fallback. Permission pre-flighting at onboarding (never mid-alarm), 2-fail fallback swap, 3-fail safe exit. Ship with three quests, not eight.

3. TITLE CARD SLAM + WAKE CARD MINT — generated episode titles (one LLM call), sunrise backgrounds, screentone filter pipeline, truth-based foil conditions, 9:16 share export. This is the share engine; it works even before audio exists.

4. MORNING EPISODE PIPELINE — overnight server-side generation (Gemini script + Flash TTS), behavior-log memory store (wake times, grace uses, missions, knockdowns) feeding callbacks, subtitle-synced player with Skip/Short Mode, zero-network morning playback. Build the anti-sameness system in from day one: rotating episode formats (standard/rival/callback/quiet), tone register variance, and a template-similarity check before audio renders.
5. TODAY STATE MACHINE + ALARM SHEET — four states with the two persistent anchors, End Credits inline night flow (stamps, mic-first mission, teaser line), TO BE CONTINUED stinger.

6. SAGA SHELF — episode count, volume spines, daily pages, knockdown/filler rendering, arc finale cards. Lightweight v1: timeline + binder grid; the 2-min finale episodes ride the existing TTS pipeline.
7. LOCK IN — chips, instant player, free/premium gating, Dynamic Island focus timer. Smallest feature; do not let it grow.

8. CAST + PROTAGONIST PASS — narrator roster with live personalized samples, RevenueCat integration, Pilot Arc trial flow, post-Episode-3 paywall trigger, free-tier weekly-episode degradation (never full memory shutoff).
9. AMBIENT SURFACES — lock-screen Live Activity countdown + ember, StandBy clock, home widget. Pure retention polish; ship after the loop proves itself.

10. HAPTIC/SOUND SCORE + KNOCKDOWN/COMEBACK SYSTEM TUNING — AHAP compositions, foley pass, recovery-day economy balancing from real cohort data, rival tone guardrails. Then onboard to TestFlight with the 16–26 anime-native cohort and tune episode writing against real day-14 fatigue before any paid acquisition.
